title: Neurologists
description: Diagnose, manage, and treat disorders and diseases of the brain, spinal cord, and peripheral nerves, with a primarily nonsurgical focus.

tasks.text: Interview patients to obtain information, such as complaints, symptoms, medical histories, and family histories.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine patients to obtain information about functional status of areas, such as vision, physical strength, coordination, reflexes, sensations, language skills, cognitive abilities, and mental status.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform or interpret the outcomes of procedures or diagnostic tests, such as lumbar punctures, electroencephalography, electromyography, and nerve conduction velocity tests.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Order or interpret results of laboratory analyses of patients' blood or cerebrospinal fluid.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Diagnose neurological conditions based on interpretation of examination findings, histories, or test results.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prescribe or administer medications, such as anti-epileptic drugs, and monitor patients for behavioral and cognitive side effects.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Identify and treat major neurological system diseases and disorders, such as central nervous system infection, cranio spinal trauma, dementia, and stroke.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop treatment plans based on diagnoses and on evaluation of factors, such as age and general health, or procedural risks and costs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inform patients or families of neurological diagnoses and prognoses, or benefits, risks and costs of various treatment plans.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare, maintain, or review records that include patients' histories, neurological examination findings, treatment plans, or outcomes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Communicate with other health care professionals regarding patients' conditions and care.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Counsel patients or others on the background of neurological disorders including risk factors, or genetic or environmental concerns.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Interpret the results of neuroimaging studies, such as Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI), Single Photon Emission Computed Tomography (SPECT), and Positron Emission Tomography (PET) scans.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Determine brain death using accepted tests and procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Coordinate neurological services with other health care team activities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Refer patients to other health care practitioners as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Advise other physicians on the treatment of neurological problems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Participate in continuing education activities to maintain and expand competence.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Order supportive care services, such as physical therapy, specialized nursing care, and social services.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide training to medical students or staff members.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Supervise medical technicians in the performance of neurological diagnostic or therapeutic activities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Participate in neuroscience research activities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform specialized treatments in areas such as sleep disorders, neuroimmunology, neuro-oncology, behavioral neurology, and neurogenetics.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prescribe or administer treatments, such as transcranial magnetic stimulation, vagus nerve stimulation, and deep brain stimulation.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
